shuayb_call.title: The call of Shu'ayb
shuayb_call.introduction: Shu'ayb (شعيب عليه السلام), Jethro, is a prophet sent to the people of Madyan. The Quran says: "And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb. He said: O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other deity than Him." (7:85) The people of Madyan practiced deception in trade. Shu'ayb called them to justice and monotheism.
shuayb_call.conclusion: Shu'ayb preached with wisdom and patience. He warned of the consequences of injustice. Some believed, but the majority rejected him. He fulfilled his mission with firmness.
shuayb_call.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that the prophet calls to justice in business. Deception and fraud are grave sins.

chapters.title: The mission
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb. He said: O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other deity than Him. A proof has come to you from your Lord. So give full measure and full weight. Do not diminish people their rights." (7:85) Shu'ayb was sent to a people of merchants. They cheated on weights and measures. He called them to worship Allah and to be just.

chapters.title: The warning
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And do not spread corruption on earth after it has been set right. That is better for you, if you are believers." (7:85) Shu'ayb warned his people. Deception corrupts society. Justice ensures prosperity. He exhorted them to fear Allah and be honest.

references.content: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ
references.translation: And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb. He said: O my people! Worship Allah. Give full measure and full weight.

references.content: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ
references.translation: And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb. He said: O my people! Worship Allah. You have no other deity than Him.
references.relevance: This verse confirms the call to monotheism.

glossary.definition: Jethro in Arabic. Prophet sent to the people of Madyan.
Midian. Region in northwest Arabia. Shu'ayb's people.

shuayb_weights.title: Shu'ayb and the scales
shuayb_weights.introduction: The people of Madyan cheated on weights and measures. The Quran says: "So give full measure and full weight. Do not diminish people their rights." (7:85) Shu'ayb exhorted them to commercial justice. They sold by giving less. They bought by demanding more. It was flagrant injustice.
shuayb_weights.conclusion: Shu'ayb insisted on honesty. Deception in trade is a sin. Allah loves the just. Believers must be upright in their transactions.
shuayb_weights.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that justice in commerce is an obligation. Fraud destroys trust and attracts punishment.

chapters.title: The deception
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "O my people! Give full measure and full weight in justice. Do not diminish people their goods. And do not spread corruption on earth." (11:85) The people of Madyan were known for fraud. When they sold, they gave less. When they bought, they took more. Shu'ayb condemned them. This practice was disguised theft.

chapters.title: The order of justice
chapters.content: The Quran says: "What remains (lawful) from Allah is better for you, if you are believers. And I am not a guardian over you." (11:86) Shu'ayb ordered justice. Give full measure and full weight. Do not wrong people. Lawful gain is blessed. Unlawful gain leads to ruin.

references.content: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ
references.translation: O my people! Give full measure and full weight in justice. Do not diminish people their goods.
references.relevance: This verse orders justice in measures.

references.content: فَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ
references.translation: Give full measure and full weight. Do not diminish people their rights.

glossary.definition: Scale. Shu'ayb ordered to use the mizan with justice.
Measure. Giving full kayl is an obligation.

shuayb_rejection.title: The rejection of Shu'ayb
shuayb_rejection.introduction: The people of Madyan rejected Shu'ayb. The Quran says: "They said: O Shu'ayb! Does your prayer command you to abandon what our fathers worshipped, or to stop doing with our wealth what we want?" (11:87) They accused him of being a magician or a liar. They refused to abandon their unjust practices.
shuayb_rejection.conclusion: Shu'ayb was patient. He continued to call. He warned of punishment. But they persisted in error. Allah's decree fell upon them.
shuayb_rejection.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that rejecting the prophets leads to perdition. Those who persist in injustice will be punished.

chapters.title: The accusations
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "They said: O Shu'ayb! We do not understand much of what you say. And we consider you weak among us." (11:91) The people despised Shu'ayb. They called him weak. They refused to obey. They said: "You are only a man like us." Shu'ayb replied: "I ask for no wage. My reward is with Allah."

chapters.title: The final warning
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And O my people! Let not my disagreement with you lead you to suffer what the people of Nuh suffered, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih." (11:89) Shu'ayb warned them. Previous peoples were destroyed for rejecting their prophets. The same fate awaited them if they persisted. But they did not believe.

references.content: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَـٰٓؤُا۟
references.translation: They said: O Shu'ayb! Does your prayer command you to abandon what our fathers worshipped?
references.relevance: This verse relates the people's accusations.

references.content: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًۭا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًۭا
references.translation: They said: O Shu'ayb! We do not understand much of what you say. We consider you weak.
references.relevance: This verse describes the people's contempt.

glossary.definition: Denial, rejection. The people made takdhib to Shu'ayb.

shuayb_destruction.title: The destruction of the people of Madyan
shuayb_destruction.introduction: The people of Madyan persisted in rejection. The Quran says: "And when Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who had believed with him, by mercy from Us. And the Cry seized those who had been unjust." (11:94) Punishment fell. An earthquake or a cry destroyed them. The unjust perished.
shuayb_destruction.conclusion: Shu'ayb and the believers were saved. They left the region before the punishment. The disbelievers were annihilated. Madyan became a lesson for generations.
shuayb_destruction.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that injustice and rejection of the prophets lead to destruction. Only believers are saved.

chapters.title: The punishment
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "And the Cry seized them. They lay lifeless in their dwellings. As if they had never lived there." (11:94-95) Allah's punishment fell upon Madyan. A cry or an earthquake destroyed them. They perished in an instant. Their houses became their graves. Allah's justice was fulfilled.

chapters.title: Salvation of the believers
chapters.content: The Quran says: "We saved Shu'ayb and those who had believed with him, by mercy from Us." (11:94) Shu'ayb and the believers were spared. Allah protected them. They left before the punishment. They found refuge elsewhere. Allah's mercy covers the believers.

references.content: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا شُعَيْبًۭا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍۢ مِّنَّا وَأَخَذَتِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ
references.translation: When Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who had believed with him. The Cry seized the unjust.
references.relevance: This verse relates salvation and punishment.

references.content: فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ ۝ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ شُعَيْبًۭا كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ
references.translation: The earthquake seized them. They lay lifeless in their dwellings. As if they had never lived there.

glossary.definition: Cry. The sayha (or rajfa) destroyed the people of Madyan.
Earthquake. The rajfa fell upon the unjust.

lessons_from_shuayb.title: Lessons from the story of Shu'ayb
lessons_from_shuayb.introduction: Shu'ayb is the prophet of commercial justice. The Quran says: "And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb." (7:85) He called his people to honesty. He fought deception. His story teaches that justice in business is a religious obligation.
lessons_from_shuayb.conclusion: Shu'ayb called to justice. He was rejected. Punishment fell. The believers were saved. The story of Madyan is an eternal warning.
lessons_from_shuayb.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that deception in trade is a grave sin. The believer must be honest in all transactions.

chapters.title: Commercial justice
chapters.content: The Quran says: "Give full measure and full weight. Do not diminish people their rights." (7:85) Shu'ayb insisted on honesty. The Muslim merchant must be just. Cheating on weights, measures or prices is forbidden. Trust is sacred. Islam demands integrity in trade.

chapters.title: Consequences of injustice
chapters.content: The people of Madyan refused. They persisted in fraud. Punishment destroyed them. The Quran reminds: "And how many cities have We destroyed! Our punishment came to them by night." (7:4) Collective injustice attracts punishment. Only justice and faith ensure salvation.

references.content: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ
references.translation: And to Madyan, their brother Shu'ayb. He said: O my people! Worship Allah.

references.content: نَجَّيْنَا شُعَيْبًۭا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍۢ مِّنَّا
references.translation: We saved Shu'ayb and those who had believed with him by mercy.
references.relevance: This passage relates the salvation of the believers.

glossary.definition: Justice. Shu'ayb called to adl in commerce.
Diminution, fraud. Tabakhasa = to cheat on measures.
